symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly enlarging painful firm inflammatory nodule on the thigh

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid enlargement of a painful follicular nodule is the typical course of a furuncle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central softening with a yellow-white pointing head (mature, pointing collection)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pointing head over a softened centre indicates a mature drainable abscess — the key sign that source control is needed.
Pure cellulitis has no fluctuant, pointing centre.
Fluctuance confirms a liquefied collection suitable for incision and drainage — a clinician palpation finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain; it is inferred from the visible pointing head.

symptoms.symptom_name: Spreading peri-lesional erythema and warmth (surrounding cellulitis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with surrounding cellulitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythema extending beyond the indurated edge indicates accompanying cellulitis and raises urgency.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with surrounding cellulitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic symptoms indicate early systemic involvement; patient-reportable and, at 37.8, a low-grade fever consistent with a localised infection rather than septic shock.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with surrounding cellulitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regional lymphadenopathy indicates lymphatic spread of infection — a clinician palpation finding, sealed as answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent staphylococcal skin infections and prior MRSA

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with surrounding cellulitis, likely MRSA
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent staph and documented prior MRSA make community MRSA the likely organism and drive MRSA-active empirical antibiotic choice.

symptoms.symptom_name: No multiple coalescing loculi or draining sinus tracts

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A single locus of pointing rather than several draining sinuses distinguishes a large furuncle from a carbuncle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs — BP 128/82, HR 98, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Temp 37.8

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with surrounding cellulitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamically stable with only a low-grade temperature — argues against septic shock and supports same-day (not emergency) disposition; clinician-measured and sealed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab / pus microscopy, culture and sensitivities at the time of incision and drainage
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the organism and MRSA status and guides definitive antibiotic choice; obtained at drainage, so it presupposes in-person care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods / inflammatory markers if systemically unwell at in-person review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: May be considered in person if systemic features progress; not a telehealth deliverable.
Not required for a clinically obvious pointing abscess and not obtainable via telehealth; the pointing fluctuant lesion is a clinical diagnosis.
